title: Intriguing Light British Mirin Yorkshire Pudding
description: A light and pungent British dish featuring mirin, this wood-fired-baked Yorkshire pudding is a unique and delicious treat. It is a raw-friendly and flavorful option for any meal.
instructions: Preheat the wood-fired oven to 425 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, mirin, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, and melted butter.
Add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
Pour the batter into a 12-cup Yorkshire pudding pan.
Place the pan in the wood-fired oven and bake for 20 minutes.
Reduce the oven temperature to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove the pudding from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Serve the pudding hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
